pinout.name: VDD_HV (I/O Power)
pinout.note: 3.0-5.5V I/O supply segment (representative, not actual ball)

warnings: AI-scaffolded profile — pinout and parameters are UNVERIFIED. Confirm against the datasheet and a real read before any write.
Always read twice and compare before writing.
This lab is 3.3 V-safe: device I/O can operate at 3.3 V, but verify segment supply configuration before connecting.
Pinout is a representative subset of key signals only — the 256-ball MAPBGA full ball map is NOT enumerated here; consult the official datasheet for exact ball assignments.
High pin/ball count automotive MCU: programming requires JTAG/Nexus debug tooling, not a generic memory programmer.
Capacity/package details are best-effort and should be confirmed against the SPC5668E datasheet.

provenance.notes: SPC5668EAVMG is an STMicroelectronics SPC56 (Power Architecture e200z6/e200z0 dual-core) automotive MCU, ST's equivalent of the Freescale/NXP MPC5668 'Fado' family; the 'E' denotes an Ethernet/gateway variant. It is programmed via JTAG/Nexus, so I mapped family to mcu_internal_flash and protocol to jtag. I estimated ~2 MB on-chip code flash and a 3.0–5.5 V multi-segment I/O supply with an internal ~1.2 V core regulator, which is typical for this family. Package suffix 'MG' most likely corresponds to a 256-ball MAPBGA, but I am not fully certain of exact flash size, RAM, and the precise ball map, so confidence is low and the pinout lists only representative key signals (power, ground, reset, oscillator, JTAG/Nexus) rather than actual ball coordinates. Verify all values against the official SPC5668E datasheet.
